shorts.hook: Leadership isn't about inventing something new
shorts.content: Leadership isn't always about inventing something new — sometimes it's about noticing what's been ignored, and daring to redesign it. Two cousins spotted a gap most of us overlook in restroom access and product equity, then built a movement around it that now serves Fortune 500 companies.
shorts.title: The leadership lesson hiding in plain sight

shorts.hook: Two cousins turned an invisible problem
shorts.content: Restroom equity is about dignity, wellness, and inclusion, not just convenience. Two cousins noticed what everyone else overlooked and transformed it into a Fortune 500 solution. Sometimes the biggest opportunities are hiding in the problems we've accepted as normal.
shorts.title: How to spot million-dollar problems everyone ignores

shorts.content: Building a business with family requires clear boundaries and defined roles to maintain both relationships and success. Co-CEO partnerships can work when both leaders bring complementary strengths and shared vision. It's about defining who does what from day one.
shorts.title: The truth about working with family in business

shorts.content: Mission-driven companies can scale to Fortune 500 clients when they solve real problems with thoughtful solutions. Scaling a values-based company requires maintaining your core mission while adapting to larger market demands. Your values become your competitive advantage.
shorts.title: How mission-driven startups win Fortune 500 deals

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When Cousins Turn Restrooms Into Revolution?
titleOptions.boldStatement: These Cousins Built a Fortune 500 Business From Bathroom Problems
titleOptions.numberBased: 7 Leadership Lessons From Building a Restroom Revolution

description:
Two cousins spotted a gap most of us overlook and built a movement that's transforming restroom equity for Fortune 500 companies.
Discover how Denielle and Thyme turned an invisible problem into the UNICORN solution.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 - Introduction to the UNICORN story
5:23 - How they identified the restroom equity gap
12:45 - Building a business with family dynamics
18:30 - Scaling from startup to Fortune 500 clients
25:12 - The co-CEO partnership model
31:47 - Maintaining mission while scaling
38:20 - Leadership lessons from restroom revolution

🎯 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Leadership often starts with noticing what others overlook
• Family business requires clear boundaries and defined roles
• Mission-driven companies can scale when solving real problems
• Restroom equity is about dignity, wellness, and inclusion
• Co-CEO partnerships work with complementary strengths
• Values-based scaling maintains core mission

📝 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/redesigning-dignity-how-unicorn-is-transforming-restroom-equity
📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/redesigning-dignity-how-unicorn-is-transforming-restroom-equity

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for weekly leadership insights: https://www.www.youtube.com/@TheManageHer
🎧 LISTEN on all podcast platforms: https://themanageher.com/podcast

#leadership #womenleaders #entrepreneurship #restromequity #unicornstory #businessgrowth #missiondriven #femaleentrepreneurs #innovation #businessscaling

communityPosts.content:
What's the biggest leadership lesson from the UNICORN story? 🦄

A) Leadership starts with noticing what others overlook
B) Family businesses need clear boundaries to succeed  
C) Mission-driven companies can scale to Fortune 500
D) Co-CEO partnerships work with shared vision

Just dropped Episode 26 with Denielle and Thyme who built a restroom revolution! Their insights on transforming invisible problems into business opportunities are game-changing.

What resonates most with your leadership journey?

"Leadership isn't always about inventing something new — sometimes it's about noticing what's been ignored, and daring to redesign it." ✨

This quote from our latest episode with UNICORN's founders hit different. Two cousins spotted a gap in restroom equity that most of us walk past every day, then built a Fortune 500 solution around it.

What "invisible" problems in your industry are waiting for a leader brave enough to redesign them? 🤔

Watch their full story in Episode 26 of The Manage Her Podcast 🎧
